summary: Conjecture 11 from arXiv:2305.15585 asks whether sufficiently large chromatic number of a graph G forces, for every tournament T on the same vertex set, some out-neighbourhood G[N+_T(v)] to contain a cycle; this is the d=2 (weakest non-trivial) case of the degeneracy Question 10 from the same paper. The source paper was published in Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B 168 (2024), pp. 86–95, but no subsequent work resolving Conjecture 11 was identified through web searches covering the period up to May 2026.
search_queries: arxiv 2305.15585 chromatic number tournament out-neighbourhood cycle conjecture
tournament out-neighbourhood cycle chromatic number Girão Hendrey Illingworth Steiner graph
high chromatic number tournament out-neighbourhood acyclic cycle 2024 2025 arxiv graph theory
Girão Hendrey Savery Steiner chromatic tournament acyclic out-neighbourhood 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up paper addressing Conjecture 11 was found. The conjecture is the d=2 case of the degeneracy Question 10; the paper's main results concern the chromatic-number version (disproving Scott–Seymour), while Conjecture 11 targets the much weaker cycle-containment requirement. The paper notes that bipartite (1-degenerate) graphs appear in their counterexamples, making d=2 the first open degeneracy case. Searches also retrieved arXiv:2306.02364 (Nguyen–Seymour survey on tournament structure) and arXiv:2503.20045 (Koerts–Moore–Spirkl on cycle orientations in digraphs), neither of which was confirmed to address this specific conjecture.
